name: Trade Caravans Harassed
Description:
A wurm has been bothering our trade caravans as they travel through the mountains, asking you to come and visit her. While this is definitely a step in the right direction, I would greatly appreciate it if she left them alone and stopped terrifying them altogether. The reward is still 2500 eros. 

- Elena

theScene: You return to Jora's lair and approach the entrance to the pitch black cave.
Jora cautiously slithers out of the dark cave until she realizes it's you and gives you a wide grin as she rushes up to you.
She sweeps you up into a massive hug as her tail gleefully swishes back and forth behind her, and as you look over her shoulder you can see something, or someone, coiled up in her tail.
Jora looks at you excitedly as she pulls away from the hug.
[ThePlayerName]! Look! Look! I made a friend!
She quickly swings her tail forward, allowing you to better see who, or what, is coiled up in it.
A harpy is trapped within Jora's tail, wide eyed and trembling with terror as she stares at Jora.
Of course she is! Isn't that right Peep?
The scared harpy lets out a frightened 'eep' and nods her head vigorously, seemingly too terrified to even consider saying no to Jora.
See? I already showed her my treasure trove and everything.
I managed to be pretty lucky this time because she wasn't paying too much attention when I saw her, so I managed to sneak up on her and catch her with my tail before she could fly away!
So you're proud of me, right [ThePlayerName]? Now I won't have to try to keep you here all the time, or be lonely when you're not around! But you'd better still come by to visit...
You open your mouth to say something, but as you start to speak the harpy suddenly interrupts you with a shriek as if suddenly realizing you're there through her terror-ridden haze.
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
YOU! YOU'RE AN ADVENTURER RIGHT?! S-SAVE ME FROM HER BEFORE SHE EATS ME! PLEASSSEEEE!
The harpy starts to cry and squirm against Jora's coils as she wordlessly begs for you to save her.
H-Hey! Calm down! I'm not going to eat you, we're friends!

The harpy takes a quick look at Jora's face as she smiles in an attempt to calm her down, but only starts to struggle more at the sight of her terrifying looking teeth, and Jora looks over at you in desperation as the harpy continues to panic.
Try to calm the harpy down.
Try to get Jora to let the harpy go.

EventText.NameOfScene: Try to calm the harpy down.
theScene: You try your best to calm down the terrified harpy as she continues to writhe and wriggle in Jora's tail.
But she doesn't even seem to hear your words as she continues to beg you to save her between shallow, panicked breaths.
Ghhh... S-She was fine before you showed up [ThePlayerName]! I don't understand...

EventText.NameOfScene: Try to get Jora to let the harpy go.
theScene: You do your best to try and convince Jora to let the harpy go.
What?! No! I'll never be able to catch a harpy like this again!
Jora seems to be trying very hard to deny how the harpy is reacting to her, but as she looks over at her wailing captive a faint look of sadness crosses her eyes.
Jora whips her head around to look at you as a few tears start forming in the corners of her eyes.
Jora sighs sadly and looks down at the ground as she frees the harpy from her tail, accepting that this isn't working out how she'd hoped it would.
The harpy flutters in the air for a brief moment before crying out and crashing into the ground face first, clutching one of her wings in pain.
Jora quickly rushes towards the injured harpy with a look of concerned fear on her face, making the harpy squawk in terror and try to creep away from her.
Accuse Jora of hurting the harpy.

EventText.NameOfScene: Accuse Jora of hurting the harpy.
theScene: music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Sense Of Loss (2_04) 54bpm.mp3
Jora recoils as if struck by your words as a look of confused disbelief crosses her face.
Wh-Wha-? I-I would never... I t-thought you w-would be...
With tears streaming down her face, Jora turns and slithers back into her cave as fast as she can, and you hear the sounds of the earth being ripped apart from inside...
You use a standard healing spell you learned in adventuring school on the harpy, and she throws her wings around you in a hug and thanks you for saving her.

She tells you about how a big gust of wind smacked her into the side of the mountain, injuring her wing and forcing her to land, and the next thing she knew, a big scary monster had captured her and taken her into a dark cave.
The harpy gives you another big hug and flies off, and you decide to check on Jora...

Jora's cave is illuminated by the same white gemstone as before and seems normal aside from a gaping hole in the back that seems to on forever, but as you stand there the gemstone slowly starts to flicker and fade.
But as you look down at the gemstone you notice something horribly wrong, as Jora's treasure hoard seems to have been utterly smashed and destroyed.

The stone slab is completely shattered, and bits of rocks, gems, gold coins, and her various knickknacks are scattered about everywhere in varying degrees of brokenness, but in the center of the destruction is Jora's treasured wooden dolls, each of which has been completely crushed and thrown into the dirt.

You solemnly place Jora's note next to the shattered remains of her dolls and look around at the insides of the cave one last time as the gemstone continues to slowly fade before flickering out and covering the cave in inky darkness.

You quietly return to town, and a few days later you get your reward of eros from Elena, who seems rather glad that the caravans are no longer being attacked or harassed. She even throws in some extra eros!
But no signs of Jora have been seen since, and her cave has been abandoned...
You use a standard healing spell you learned in adventuring school on the harpy, and while it seems to fix her up decently well, her wing still doesn't look quite right.
The harpy scrabbles up from off the ground and hides behind you as Jora continues to look at her, clearly worried about her wing.
H-Hey, I didn't hurt you, did I? Peep?
MY NAME'S NOT PEEP! AND YOU WERE GOING TO {i}EAT{/i} ME! GO MIGHTY HERO! SMITE HER!
Jora wasn't going to eat you, she eats rocks. And how exactly am I supposed to smite her? With my dick?
The harpy suddenly stops shaking as her face clouds in thought, and she stares off into the distance before she blinks in realization.
H-Hey, her wing {i}really{/i} isn't looking good...
The harpy shrieks as Jora moves a little closer to try to examine her wing and cowers behind you before aggressively pointing her undamaged wing towards Jora.
AAAAAAHH! SHE'S TRYING TO EAT MY WING! SHE'S GOING TO COVER IT IN SAUCE AND COOK IT AND THEN SHE'S GOING TO EAT ME!
Uhm... I'm going to go grab something from my cave... To fix her wing...
SHE'S GETTING THE SAUCE! GO HERO! SMITE HER! SMITE HER WITH YOUR DICK!
Jora snickers at the harpy's words, which only makes her more and more frantic as she tightens her grip on your back, and you give Jora a dirty look as she slithers back into her cave.

The harpy suddenly stops trembling as Jora disappears into the cave, and as you turn to look at her, the harpy is staring up at you with a look of resigned acceptance as she mutters the names of her friends and family back at her nest, clearly believing that she'll never see them again.

As you're shaking the harpy and trying to tell her that she isn't going to die here today, she suddenly freezes again, and you turn around to see Jora coming back out of the cave with a small, multi colored rock in her hand as she slithers towards the both of you.
Here, it's a rejuvenation stone, it'll fix you up! I promise.

Jora tries to give the harpy a reassuring smile, but at the sight of her teeth the harpy starts to tremble in fear. Instead of hiding behind you, however, she looks towards you as you tell her that she'll be fine, and her face steels itself with resolve as she looks back towards Jora.
The harpy tentatively reaches out her injured wing, wincing slightly in pain as she slowly inches towards Jora.
Jora carefully taps the stone to the harpy's wing, and as the rock suddenly dissolves, the harpy lets out a surprised squawk as her wing starts to repair itself before her very eyes.
She hops out from behind you and gives her wing a few experimental flaps before she bounds over to Jora, apparently no longer scared of her as she excitedly hops around her tail.
That's so cooool! You're not evil are you? You're the best wurm ever!
Before Jora can respond, the harpy flies up and tackle hugs her in the face.
You have to try to hold back laughter at the ridiculous sight of the petite bird woman firmly attached to Jora's head while Jora freezes in shock.
Jora raises a claw, and her muffled voice asks.
Is... Is she my friend now?
The harpy flutters down and starts to hop back and forth energetically.
Where did you get that awesome rock?
Underground? That's where I find all my gems and stuff...
Jora awkwardly stands in place as the harpy continues to rapidly zip around her, clearly unsure on how to deal with her hyper personality.
If you have any others, will you trade for them? What about any other shiny gems you find? I can trade some other cool stuff for them if you want!
Huh?! But they're mine! And besides, now that you're my friend you have to stay here!
Jora crosses her arms with a sense of finality as the harpy stops in front of her.
E-Eh?! B-But I have stuff to do!
Kidnapping isn't the same as being friends, and you can trade the rocks you don't like for things you might want Jora...
W-Well, I guess... but... Ugh... Fine! I'll trade off some of the things I don't like as much if you want... But... You'll still visit even if it's not about that... right?
Of course! You're so cool! I'll even bring a bunch of my harpy friends to come meet you too! They'll never believe that wurms could be so nice! It'll be great!
Well... maybe I'll just bring a few of them at a time then, is that all right?
Jora can only nod as she stands there, still trying to process everything that's going on.
Alright, awesome! I'd love to stick around for longer, but I'm already really late for a delivery I have to make, so I'll see you later!
The harpy starts to fly off.
Uh... Okay, see you later Peep...

The harpy turns around in midair and starts to wave goodbye to the two of you, almost crashing into the ground as she twists and tries to recover before flashing the two of you an embarrassed smile and disappearing down the side of the mountain.
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
Jora stares at where the harpy disappeared with a forlorn look on her face.
A momentary silence comes between the two of you as numerous sad expressions flit across Jora's face, and the only sound is that of the wind whistling across the mountain...
Jora jerks out of her reverie at the sound of your voice and looks over at you with an emotionally unguarded expression for a split second before staring down at the ground as she recollects herself.

EH?! A-Ah... I almost forgot you were there [ThePlayerName]... Well... I won't be bothering the caravans anymore. A-And I don't mean as in I won't attack them, I really do mean I won't go after them anymore. For sure this time.
Jora fidgets with her claws for a bit as she mulls something over, then she sighs and looks you in the eyes with a sad, serious look.
[ThePlayerName]...? I... I just wanted to say... thanks.
Not just for helping me make a friend, but just... in general.
Jora lets out another sigh and wraps her tail around her body as her voice gets much more subdued.
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Sense Of Loss (2_04) 54bpm.mp3
Before you showed up, I was... not doing so well.

I just spent all my time digging around underground for a long time, but eventually everything just seemed so... worthless. I got so bored of everything that even waking up sometimes felt like too much effort every day.

Honestly [ThePlayerName]? I even had trouble getting out of bed most days. The only time where I ever felt like it was worth it is if I felt someone approaching my cave, so I tried to make other people notice me so I'd feel... something, anything other than just the overwhelming nothingness, but the looks of fear and hate in their eyes only ended up making me feel worse...
If... I-If it wasn't for you, I probably would have just kept doing that until s-something r-really bad would have happened to me, a-and then...
music/Emotional/Purple Planet Music - Emotional - Spiritual Moment (1_32) 90bpm (L).mp3
Jora trails off and starts to gently rub the tears forming in the corners of her eyes with a claw before she gives you a very faint teary smile.
Jora straightens herself with a sniffle and starts to turn back towards her cave.
I-I'll be right back... Th-There's something in my eyes and... I want to give you something... So... So stay right there!
She quickly heads into her cave, and it doesn't take her too long to return with something carefully held in her claws.
Jora motions for you to hold out your arms and gently places something in your hands before slowly backing up with a sad little smile.
It's an aged wooden doll of a brave looking knight, and you immediately recognize it as one of the items from her treasure hoard.
I want you to have it... J-Just don't think too hard about it, and take it...
You carefully stow the doll in your bag.

So... Just remember [ThePlayerName], you're my precious friend no matter what. Even if I end up swarmed with 50 harpies a day, or some other species of monster, I want you to know that you are my first and best friend.
Despite any new friends I make, I always want you to know that.
And despite the fact that you probably took that quest for the reward initially, you'll still come visit me from time to time, right? At least every once in a while...?
Jora glares at you with a pout and crosses her arms.
...Jerk. Well if you're going to be like that after all of this... Maybe I {i}might{/i} attack some caravans.
She sticks her tongue out at you.
Heh, but I probably won't. Hm, and here I was, thinking we could be a bit more than just 'friends'... Feel free to come back any time and change that {i}maybe{/i}.
She returns to her cave, slightly miffed by your answer, leaving you to head back to town.
music/Mountain/Purple Planet Music - Chilled - Desert Winds (3_12).mp3
Jora smiles widely and her tail swishes back and forth a little, unconsciously destroying a few large rocks as she practically quivers with happiness.
Hmhmhm, of course you'd want to come back! You'd probably get depressed if you went without basking in my draconic presence every once in a while!
Jora suddenly looks away shyly with a slight blush.
But uh... I-If you wanted, we could be a bit more than just friends... So uhm... feel free to think on that.
She quickly returns to her cave with a deep flush, leaving you to head back to town.
music/Mountain/Purple Planet Music - Chilled - Desert Winds (3_12).mp3
Jora looks confused as you reach your hand out towards her, which only intensifies as you start to pat her head reassuringly.
Jora glares at you with an embarrassed flush, and then gently swats your hand away with a claw.
You could have just said you'll visit, you know, or you could have just kissed me instead...
Jora suddenly straightens as her face turns bright crimson.
W-What I mean is... Uhm... We could be more than just friends... Ah... Feel free to think on that.
She quickly returns to her cave with a deep flush, leaving you to head back to town.
music/Mountain/Purple Planet Music - Chilled - Desert Winds (3_12).mp3
You take a few steps forwards and pull Jora into a deep kiss, much to her surprise.
For a few moments she's stunned, and you easily dominate her mouth with your tongue, eliciting a few soft moans from her.
Jora suddenly and roughly pushes you backwards with her mouth still pressed against yours, and you soon find your back pinned against her large tail as she aggressively kisses you back.
Her golden eyes gaze into yours with burning intensity as your tongues passionately intertwine.
While both of you struggle for dominance in the make out session, Jora's long tail slowly coils around you and starts to grind against your groin.
You can only groan and gasp into Jora's mouth as her tail teases your hardening manhood, and she takes advantage of your momentary distraction to continue to kiss you senseless.

Jora continues to press her advantage as her eyes blaze with lust, completely dominating your mouth as her tail continues to play with your cock, but her eyes suddenly clear and she pulls away from you, making your shaft throb with denial as you catch your breath.
Ah! Uhm. Sorry, I got a little carried away...
Jora fidgets a little as her face turns completely red.
I like you too [ThePlayerName], I'm glad you feel that way too... I really won't mind being more than friends, but uh, I think I need some time to think on all the stuff that's happened.
S-So be sure to come back later!
She quickly returns to her cave with a deep flush and a smile, leaving you to head back to town while your cock still aches from her teasing.
After waiting a while once getting back to town, you check in at the guild hall to see if the quest is, in fact, completed.

Strangely, you had to hand in the letter from Jora about her making a friend as evidence of quest completion, but seeing as you have a much more sentimental gift from the quest you don't fuss over it much.
As you enter the guild hall the next day, Elena approaches you with an exuberant look on her face.
[ThePlayerName]! You did it! The caravan attacks finally stopped! I don't care how you did it, but you have saved me from having to do {i}so much paperwork!{/i} Thank you! Thank you so much!
Elena gleefully hands you a hefty bag of eros, there seems to be more than the reward initially stated!
After handing you the money, Elena wistfully stares off into the distance as a faint smile appears on her lips.
Ahhh... Maybe to celebrate tonight I'll have a glass of wine...
You think you hear Elly mutter behind you, something about 'the whole bottle'. But you can't be sure she actually said anything.
Go see Elena at the Guild counter.
With the quest complete and the reward secured, you don't have to go see Jora anymore, though she would probably like to see you again anyway.
Go see Elena at the Guild counter.
